description: Create a two-column content slide on precision data and customer context, with explanatory text on the left and a right-hand sidebar featuring a donut chart and stacked bar chart on executive and consumer attitudes toward gen AI.

header.kicker: Technology Vision 2025 | AI: A Declaration of Autonomy
header.headline: Precision data and customer context

items.sub: Precision data is key to businesses’ digital customer strategies today. It fuels the ability to deliver more relevant deals, contextualized offerings, and more. Right now, though, businesses are stuck between customers that demand more relevance, and a regulatory and social environment that is limiting the ways they typically gather that data.

Microsoft Dynamics 365 is a platform that uses AI to help companies turn their customer data and the data across their organization into customizable, generative AI experiences. According to Microsoft, this platform can leverage real-time insights about people to better personalize customer journeys and to help sellers close more deals.

items.text: How does it connect to the trend?
items.sub: Personified AI can be a major reinvention of how businesses gather useful customer data safely and effectively. Through interactions with AI, businesses have the opportunity to get to know customers beyond purchase history and demographics—useful data, like their interests, needs, or even conversation style can be gleaned from every interaction. What’s more is AI itself can be used to get consent in the moment, by asking the user for access to data that can create more relevant outcomes—access that can be removed and data forgotten upon the termination of the conversation.

items.text: Executives are starting to understand the importance of customer context to personified AI. For personified AI to create meaningful customer interactions, it needs new types of customer context. Demographic information and purchase history are not enough—the best AI will also leverage past conversations to inform future ones.
76% of executives agree that conversational interactions using gen AI will become a way to gather relevant customer context.
Consumers who are familiar with gen AI feel more positive (74%) about the use of gen AI for marketing and advertising than consumers who are less familiar with gen AI (42%).

labels: Consumers less familiar with gen AI
main.title: Overall, how do you feel about the use of Gen AI for marketing and advertising?

footer.source: Sources: Accenture Technology Vision 2025 Executive Survey, N=4,021; Accenture Consumer Paradoxes Survey, Oct 2024, N=12,215; Consumers less familiar with gen AI, N=4,389; Consumers familiar with gen AI, N=7,826
